blocks:
Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem. (1)
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۲)
The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (2)
مَا خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ عَمَّآ أُنذِرُوا۟ مُعْرِضُونَ (۳)
We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them except with consummate wisdom and for a specified term. Yet the faithless are disregardful of what they are warned. (3)

Key Points

The beginning of the seven Surahs—Ghafir, Fussilat, Shura, Zukhruf, Dukhan, Jathiyah, and Al-Ahqaf—is the disjointed letters "حم" (Ha Mim), which are known as the "Seven Ha Mims" (Hawaamim Sab'ah). From the repetition of the Ayah "حم، تنزیل الكتاب..." (Ha Mim, The revelation of the Book...) in several consecutive Surahs, we can understand that important and fundamental issues must be repeated.

Izzah (Might/Glory) is one of the attributes and perfections mentioned for Allah, "من اللّه العزیز" (from Allah, the All-Mighty), for the Book of Allah, "انّه لكتاب عزیز"(2) (Indeed, it is a glorious Book), and for the Prophet of Allah and the believers in Allah. "انّ العزّة للّه و لرسوله و للمؤمنین"(3) (Indeed, all might belongs to Allah, and to His Messenger, and to the believers).

The creation of existence is wise and based on truth (Haqq). If we encounter something in existence whose secrets we do not understand, this does not cast doubt upon its Creator; rather, the problem lies in the limitation of our knowledge, as we read elsewhere: "و ما خلقنا السّموات و الارض و ما بینهما لاعبین، ما خلقناهما الاّ بالحقّ و لكن أكثرهم لایعلمون"(4) (And We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them in play. We did not create them except with truth, but most of them do not know).

By paying close attention to the verses that follow the disjointed letters "حم" (Ha Mim), we conclude that the Quran: 1. is from the source of Allah's knowledge and might. "تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز العلیم"(5) 2. is from the source of His mercy and affection. "تنزیل من الرّحمن الرحیم"(6) 3. is from the source of His power and wisdom. "كذلك یوحى... اللّه العزیز الحكیم"(7) 4. is a clear and clarifying Book, a source of thought and intellect. "الكتاب المبین... قرآناً عربیّاً لعلّكم تعقلون"(8) 5. is a blessed Book and a source of blessing. "و هذا كتاب أنزلناه مبارك فاتّبعوه"(9)

One of the fundamental factors leading to human deviation is the failure to pay attention to the limited and transient nature of the world, coupled with the illusion of one's own permanence. Therefore, as stated by the Quran, man resorts to various ways and methods to make himself immortal: 1. Sometimes, for his survival, he engages in accumulating wealth and counting and calculating it, failing to give any charity. "الّذى جمع مالاً و عدّده . یحسب انّ ماله اخلده"(10) 2. Sometimes, for his immortality, he constructs strong buildings. "و تتّخذون مصانع لعلّكم تخلدون"(11) 3. Sometimes, for his permanence, he turns to tyranny, mobilization, and power-seeking. "واستكبر هو و جنوده فى الارض بغیر الحقّ و ظنّوا انّهم الینا لایرجعون"(12) 4. Sometimes, in his own delusion, he attempts to confront death and Divine wrath. "وظنّوا انّهم مانعتهم حصونهم"(13)

He is heedless that the limitation of life is a reality that applies to everyone. "اینما تكونوا یدرككم الموت و لو كنتم فى بروج مشیّدة"(14) (Wherever you may be, death will overtake you, even if you should be within fortified towers). And the Quran has always reminded man of the danger of heedlessness. "ما خلقنا السّموات و الارض و ما بینهما الاّ بالحقّ و أجل مسمّى" (We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except with truth and for a specified term).

2) Fussilat, 41. 3) Al-Munafiqun, 8. 4) Ad-Dukhan, 38-39. 5) Ghafir, 2. 6) Fussilat, 2. 7) Ash-Shura, 3. 8) Az-Zukhruf, 2-3. 9) Al-An'am, 155. 10) Al-Humazah, 2-3. 11) Ash-Shu'ara, 129. 12) Al-Qasas, 39. 13) Al-Hashr, 2. 14) An-Nisa, 78.

Messages

  1. The revelation of the Quran is possible only from Allah, the All-Mighty and All-Wise, and is beyond the capacity of man. "تنزیل الكتاب من اللّه العزیز الحكیم"
  2. Although the programs of the Quran are heavenly and sublime, they have been revealed commensurate with the limited thought and capacity of man. "تنزیل الكتاب"
  3. Since the revelation of the Quran is from Allah, Who is the source of might (Izzah) and wisdom, its programs and commands guide the individual and society toward might and steadfastness. "من اللّه العزیز الحكیم"
  4. The might and power of Allah are combined with wisdom and competence. "اللّه العزیز الحكیم"
  5. A purposeful system cannot exist without a plan. Creation (Takwin) and legislation (Tashri') are based on truth (Haqq) and wisdom. "الكتاب... الحكیم - خلقنا... بالحق"
  6. In the system of creation, there is no place for crookedness, deviation, weakness, baseness, falsehood, or futility. "ما خلقنا السّموات و الارض و ما بینهما الا بالحق"
  7. The heavens, the earth, and all of existence have a calculated and measured time and end; they are neither accidental nor without purpose. "خلقنا... بالحق و أجل مسمى"
  8. The necessity of the truthfulness of creation is the existence of a world after it. (If there were no Resurrection, many inequalities and injustices would remain uncompensated and unjustified.) "ما خلقنا... الاّ بالحقّ و أجل مسمّى"
  9. All phenomena, from beginning to end, are calculated and monitored. "أجل مسمّى"
  10. Attention to the limitations of the world and the instability of blessings and phenomena is the basis for truth-seeking, detachment from the world, and a warning for man. "أجل مسمّى"
  11. The system of creation (Takwin) and legislation (Tashri') is for the guidance and perfection of man; nevertheless, some show ingratitude and commit disbelief (Kufr). "والّذین كفروا عما انذروا معرضون"
  12. All of existence moves along the path of truth (Haqq), and it is man who can deviate by choosing an untrue path. "والّذین كفروا عمّا انذروا معرضون"
  13. In the system of education, training, and propagation, warning and threat are necessary. "اُنذروا"
  14. Turning away from the truth (Haqq) is a sign of man's will and choice. "معرضون"

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Ahqaf

This Surah, which is located at the beginning of the twenty-sixth Juz' (part) of the Quran, has thirty-five verses and was revealed in Mecca. Al-Ahqaf means shifting sands that accumulate due to the wind. The land of the people of 'Ad was called Al-Ahqaf because it was a sandy and desert region. The naming of this Surah as "Al-Ahqaf" is based on Ayah 21, which recounts the story of Prophet Hud and the people of 'Ad. This Surah is the last of the seven Surahs that begin with the disjointed letters "حم" (Ha Mim), and in all of them, following these letters, the discussion concerns the revelation of the Quran and its importance.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever recites Surah Al-Ahqaf every Friday night will be safe from worldly fear and terror in this world, and will have no fear in the Hereafter either.(1)

1) Tafsir Ithna Ashari.

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ أَرُونِى مَاذَا خَلَقُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ ۖ ٱئْتُونِى بِكِتَـٰبٍ مِّن قَبْلِ هَـٰذَآ أَوْ أَثَـٰرَةٍ مِّنْ عِلْمٍ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤)
Say, ‘Tell me about those you invoke besides Allah. Show me what [part] of the earth have they created. Do they have any share in the heavens? Bring me a scripture [revealed] before this, or some vestige of [divine] knowledge, if you are truthful.’ (4)

Key Points

'Athārah' means remaining traces and signs of the existence of something. The polytheists also accepted that the Creator of the heavens and the earth is Allah. "و لئن سئلتهم من خلق السّموات و الارض لیقولن اللّه" (15) Therefore, Allah reproaches them for why they turn to idols and others, worshipping them instead of God and seeking help from them. The logic of Monotheism (Tawhid) is so strong and well-reasoned that one can use it to argue with the polytheists and condemn them. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of 'Kitāb' (Book) in this Ayah is the Torah and the Gospel, and the meaning of 'Ilm' (knowledge) is the sayings of the successors of the prophets. (16)

15) Luqman, 25. 16) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Prophet is obedient to Allah in all aspects of his mission, even in the method of arguing with opponents. "قل..." (Say...)

2- In propagation and guidance, one can awaken the innate nature (Fitrah) of others through questioning and prompt them to contemplation and thought. "أرأیتم... ماذا خلقوا"

3- Steadfastness and perseverance are essential conditions for propagation; do not cease propagating even after the disbelievers turn away. "معرضون قل أرأیتم..."

4- In accepting beliefs, do not suffice with the sayings of others; accept what you yourselves have seen and understood. "أرایتم... ارونى"

5- An entity is qualified to be invoked and called upon only if it possesses the power of creation. The root of Monotheism in worship is Monotheism in creatorship. "ما تدعون... ماذا خلقوا"

6- Idols and imaginary deities are utterly incapable; they have neither a role in the creation of the earth, "ماذا خلقوا من الارض" nor a share in establishing the heavens. "أم لهم شرك فى السّموات"

7- In arguing and conversing with opponents, one must rely on common ground. (The polytheists accepted the creatorship of Allah.) "أم لهم شرك فى السّموات"

8- The Divine Books preceding the Quran are worthy of respect and trust. "ایتونى بكتاب من قبل هذا"

9- In argumentation and reasoning, the proof must either be innate and intuitive, "أرأیتم" (Have you considered?), or transmitted, "بكتاب" (with a Book), or rational, "اثارة من علم" (a trace of knowledge); and polytheism has no proof whatsoever.

10- The presentation and acceptance of any claim must be logical and based on knowledge and reasoning. "ایتونى بكتاب... أو اثارة من علم"

11- Memories and mementos, relics of the past, and cultural heritage are valuable only when they contain a sign of knowledge and growth. "اثارة من علم"

Ayahs

وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّن يَدْعُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَن لَّا يَسْتَجِيبُ لَهُۥٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَهُمْ عَن دُعَآئِهِمْ غَـٰفِلُونَ (٥)
Who is more astray than him who invokes besides Allah such [entities] as would not respond to him until the Day of Resurrection, and who are oblivious of their invocation? (5)
وَإِذَا حُشِرَ ٱلنَّاسُ كَانُوا۟ لَهُمْ أَعْدَآءً وَكَانُوا۟ بِعِبَادَتِهِمْ كَـٰفِرِينَ (٦)
When mankind are mustered [on Judgement’s Day] they will be their enemies, and they will disavow their worship. (6)

Key Points

The greatest deviation is deviation in recognition (of God), and whoever abandons the All-Knowing, All-Powerful, All-Hearing, All-Seeing God and turns to others besides Him has fallen into such a deviation, because others besides God have no power to do anything. Similar to Ayah 5 is Ayah 29 of Surah Yunus, which states that the idols of the polytheists will say to them on the Day of Judgment: «فكفى باللّه شهیداً بیننا و بینكم ان كنّا عن عبادتكم لغافلین» (Allah is sufficient as a witness between us and you that we were unaware of your worship.) How great is the difference between a deity who hears and answers the prayer, «اُدعونى استجب لكم»(17) and a deity who never hears and never responds. «مَن لایستجیب له» Or, even if they hear, they lack the power to respond! «ان تدعوهم لایسمعوا دعائكم و لو سمعوا ما استجابوا لكم»(18)

The difference in the role of God and others besides God in human life

A: The Role of God in Human Life 1. He is his Creator. «خلق الانسان»(19) 2. He is the Lord of him and all the worlds. «ربّ العالمین»(20) 3. He is very kind to him. «بالناس لرؤف»(21) 4. He hears his prayer. «سمیع الدعاء»(22) 5. He is his Helper in distress. «یجیب المضطر»(23) 6. He guides him well. «الّذین جاهدوا فینا لنهدینّهم سبلنا»(24) 7. He supports and protects him. «و هو یتولّى الصالحین»(25) 8. He grants him growth and perfection. «یهدى الى الرّشد»(26) 9. He grants him tranquility and assurance. «ألا بذكر اللّه تطمئنّ القلوب»(27) 10. He teaches him. «الّذى علّم بالقلم»(28) 11. He grants him abundance and increase. «لئن شكرتم لازیدنّكم»(29) 12. He cures his illness. «و اذا مرضت فهو یشفین»(30) 13. He is his hope for the future. «والّذى أطمع أن یغفرلى خطیئتى یوم الدین»(31)

B: The Role of Others Besides God in Human Life 1. They have no power in creation. «لن یخلقوا ذُباباً»(32) 2. They are unaware of human requests. «و هم عن دعائهم غافلون»(33) 3. They do not hear his prayer. «لایسمعوا دعائكم»(34) 4. They lack the power to respond. «یدعوا من دون اللّه من لایستجیب له» (Ahqaf, 5) 5. They are enemies of man. «كانوا لهم اعداء» (Ahqaf, 6) 6. They are powerless against calamities. «لایملكون كشف الضرّ عنكم»(35) 7. They have neither the power to benefit nor to prevent harm. «ما لایضرّهم و لاینفعهم»(36)

And in summary, according to Prophet Yusuf عليه السلام: «أأرباب متفرّقون خیر أم اللّه الواحد القهّار»(37) Are scattered and various gods better, or Allah, the One, the Irresistible?

17) Ghafir, 60. 18) Fatir, 14. 19) Ar-Rahman, 3. 20) Hamd, 2. 21) Baqarah, 143. 22) Aal-Imran, 38. 23) Naml, 62. 24) Ankabut, 69. 25) A'raf, 196. 26) Jinn, 2. 27) Ra'd, 28. 28) Alaq, 4. 29) Ibrahim, 7. 30) Shu'ara, 80. 31) Shu'ara, 82. 32) Hajj, 73. 33) Ahqaf, 5. 34) Fatir, 14. 35) Isra, 56. 36) Yunus, 18. 37) Yusuf, 39.

Messages

1- Intellectual and doctrinal deviations are the greatest deviations of mankind. «من أضلّ»

2- Any path other than the path of Allah is misguidance. «مَن اضل ممّن یدعوا من دون اللّه»

3- People turn to others besides God for worldly matters, while those others not only fail to help in this world but will also become enemies on the Day of Judgment. «لایستجیب له... كانوا لهم اعداء»

4- Non-divine and polytheistic worldly ties will turn into enmity and hostile relationships on the Day of Judgment. «اذا حشر النّاس كانوا لهم أعداء»

5- Requesting from others, if done instead of requesting from Allah, is a type of worship and devotion that is incompatible with Monotheism (Tawhid). «دعائهم... بعبادتهم»

6- Imaginary deities are unaware of the worship of their followers in this world and will deny it on the Day of Judgment. «عن دعائهم غافلون، بعبادتهم كافرین»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَهُمْ هَـٰذَا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, the faithless say of the truth when it comes to them: ‘This is plain magic.’ (7)

Key Points

The word "mubīn" (clear/manifest) indicates the extraordinary impact of the speech and miracles of the prophets, to the extent that people mistake it for magic and sorcery. When the deniers heard the verses of the Quran, they had various statements and reactions, including: 1. We have heard these before; if we wished, we could say something similar. «اذا تتلى علیهم آیاتنا قالوا قد سمعنا و لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا»(38) 2. Remove or change the verses that negate and reject the idols so that we accept the Quran. «ائت بقرآن غیر هذا او بدّله»(39) 3. What is important is wealth and possessions, nothing else. «قال الّذین كفروا... اىّ الفریقین خیرٌ مقاماً»(40) 4. They turned away arrogantly. «قد كانت آیاتى تتلى علیكم و كنتم على اعقابكم تنكصون»(41) 5. They denied it. «ألم تكن آیاتى تتلى عليكم فكنتم بها تكذّبون»(42) 6. They feigned deafness and hearing impairment. «اذا تتلى علیه آیاتنا ولّى مستكبراً كأن لم یسمعها»(43) 7. They considered it slander and innovation. «قالوا ما هذا الاّ رجل یرید ان یصدّكم عما كان یعبد آبائكم و قالوا ما هذا الاّ افك مفترى»(44) 8. They had opposition and reactions based on no evidence and false fanaticism. «ما كان حجّتهم الا أن قالوا ائتوا بآبائنا»(45) 9. They claimed that the verses of the Quran were myths and lacked reality. «اذا تتلى علیه آیاتنا قال أساطیر الاولین»(46) 10. They called the message and the Prophet of Allah, magic and a magician. «قال الّذین كفروا للحقّ لمّا جائهم هذا سحر مبین» (Al-Ahqaf, 7)

38) Al-Anfal, 31. 39) Yunus, 15. 40) Maryam, 73. 41) Al-Mu'minun, 66. 42) Al-Mu'minun, 105. 43) Luqman, 7. 44) Saba, 43. 45) Al-Jathiyah, 25. 46) Al-Qalam, 15.

Messages

1- If the spirit of obstinacy prevails, even clear signs are denied. «و اذا تتلى علیهم آیاتنا بیّنات قال الّذین كفروا»

2- The verses of the Holy Quran are clear and illuminating. «آیاتنا بیّنات»

3- The miracles of the prophets were unbelievable to the dark-hearted, yet they were remarkable and extraordinary. «هذا سحر مبین»

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ إِنِ ٱفْتَرَيْتُهُۥ فَلَا تَمْلِكُونَ لِى مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۖ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۖ كَفَىٰ بِهِۦ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۸)
Or they say, ‘He has fabricated it.’ Say, ‘Should I have fabricated it, you would not avail me anything against Allah. He best knows what you gossip concerning it. He suffices as a witness between me and you, and He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.’ (8)

Key Points

The word "tufīḍūn" (تُفیضون) is derived from "ifāḍah" (افاضه), meaning entering, going inside, overflowing, or pouring forth. If someone falsely claims prophethood, it is incumbent upon Allah to expose him, and no one can prevent his disgrace or defend him against Allah.

Messages

1- Obstinacy and lack of piety lead to various contradictory reactions and conflicting statements. (In the previous Ayah, they called the Quran magic, and in this Ayah, they call the Prophet a fabricator of lies). "hādha siḥr... iftarāh" (هذا سحر... افتراه)

2- Do not leave doubts unanswered; respond to them. "yaqūlūn... qul" (یقولون... قل)

3- Slander against Allah entails a punishment and destruction that nothing can prevent. "in iftaraytuhu falā tamlikūna lī mina Allāhi shay’an" (ان افتریته فلا تملكون لى من اللّه شیئاً)

4- Every word the enemy utters is motivated by corruption. "yaqūlūna iftarāh... tufīḍūna fīh" (یقولون افتراه... تفیضون فیه)

5- Belief in the presence of Allah can be both the best means of refraining from transgression and the support and hope for the believers. "kafā bihi shahīdan" (كفى به شهیدا)

6- In education (or moral training), we should follow the path of moderation and present fear and hope side by side. "Huwa a‘lamu bimā tufīḍūn... wa Huwa al-Ghafūru ar-Raḥīm" (هو اعلم بما تفیضون... و هو الغفور الرحیم)

7- Firmness alongside compassion is valuable. "falā tamlikūna lī mina Allāhi... wa Huwa al-Ghafūru ar-Raḥīm" (فلا تملكون لى من اللّه... و هو الغفور الرحیم)

8- The path of repentance is open even for the disbelievers and deniers of the message. "Huwa a‘lamu bimā tufīḍūn... wa Huwa al-Ghafūru ar-Raḥīm" (هو اعلم بما تفیضون... و هو الغفور الرحیم)

Ayah

قُلْ مَا كُنتُ بِدْعًا مِّنَ ٱلرُّسُلِ وَمَآ أَدْرِى مَا يُفْعَلُ بِى وَلَا بِكُمْ ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۹)
Say, ‘I am not a novelty among the apostles, nor do I know what will be done with me, or with you. I just follow whatever is revealed to me, and I am just a manifest warner.’ (9)

Key Points

Bid‘ (بِدع) means a newly emerging and unprecedented phenomenon. The opponents dealt with the Prophet in such a way as if no prophet had ever come before, and this matter was a novelty. The Prophet is prepared for and informed of the unseen (ghayb) only what Allah grants Him, «تلك من انباء الغیب نوحیها الیك»(47), and without divine grant, revelation, and teaching, no one knows about the unseen or the future. As the Quran states: «فلا یظهر على غیبه احداً الاّ مَن ارتضى من رسول»(48) The Prophet speaks nothing but revelation, «ما یَنطقُ عن الهوى اِن هو الاّ وحىٌ یوحى »(49), and in action, He follows nothing but revelation, «ان اتّبع الا ما یوحى الىّ». Therefore, the speech and conduct (sirah) of the Prophet are a proof (hujjah) and a model for the people.

47) Hud, 49. 48) Al-Jinn, 26 - 27. 49) An-Najm, 3 - 4.

Messages

1- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is commissioned to respond to doubts (shubuhat). «قل...»

2- Divine leaders act according to Allah’s command, even in the manner of defending their own truthfulness. «قل ما كنتُ...»

3- The commissioning (bi‘that) and mission (risalah) of the prophets have been a continuous process throughout history. «ما كنت بدعاً من الرسل»

4- Doubts must be answered with logic and moderation. The opponents said: «سحر مبین... افتراه» (manifest magic... he fabricated it), but the Prophet said: «كفى باللّه شهیداً... ما كنتُ بدعاً من الرسل» (Allah suffices as a witness... I am not a novelty among the Messengers).

5- Since people show sensitivity toward new and emerging issues, stating the background and past history is necessary. «قل ما كنتُ بدعاً من الرسل»

6- The requirement of prophethood is not knowing everything. «ما ادرى»

7- A leader must speak honestly with the people. «و ما ادرى ما یفعل بى ولا بكم»

8- When stating shared matters, start with yourself first, then others. «ما یفعل بى ولا بكم»

9- The Prophet's duty is not to compel people to believe; His duty is to warn and announce danger. «ما أنا الاّ نذیر مبین»

10- For heedless people, warning is more necessary than giving hope. «ما أنا الاّ نذیر مبین»

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ وَكَفَرْتُم بِهِۦ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِدٌ مِّنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ عَلَىٰ مِثْلِهِۦ فَـَٔامَنَ وَٱسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٠)
Say, ‘Tell me, if it is from Allah and you disbelieve in it, and a witness from the Children of Israel has testified to its like and believed [in it], while you are disdainful [of it]?’ Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (10)

Key Points

Removing arrogance is necessary in many cases. Just as the Quran addresses the believers in one place, saying: Whoever among you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring others who are better than you. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا من یرتدّ منكم عن دینه فسوف یأتى اللّه بقوم یحبّهم و یحبّونه»(50) And in another place, He addresses the wives of the Prophet, saying: Do not make excuses, if the Prophet divorces you, He will be granted wives better than you. «عسى ربّه اِن طلّقكنّ أن یُبدله أزواجاً خیراً منكنّ»(51) In this verse too, He addresses the polytheists of Mecca, saying: Before you, the Children of Israel testified to the truthfulness of the Torah and believed in it. If you commit disbelief and refuse to accept it, the Quran will not be harmed; you should worry about the consequence of your own actions. Most commentators have said that the intent of the "witness" (شاهد) in «شهد شاهد» is the famous Jewish scholar, Abdullah ibn Salam, who testified to the truthfulness of Islam and the Prophet of Islam in the presence of the Jews.

50) Ma'idah, 54. 51) Tahrim, 5.

Messages

  1. Man must sometimes reflect upon his methods and stances. If he has arrogantly refused to accept the call of truth, what answer will he have for his arrogance? «أرأیتم»

  2. When speaking with opponents, do not show fanaticism regarding your beliefs, so that the ground for free discussion is prepared. Although the Prophet is certain that the Quran is from Allah, He still says: «ان كان من عند اللّه»

  3. We should honor individuals who break the mold (or break ranks) in an environment of deviation. «و شهد شاهد»

  4. The root of disbelief (kufr) is arrogance (istikbar). «آمن و استكبرتم» (Instead of saying «كفرتم» [you disbelieved], He says: «استكبرتم» [you acted arrogantly]).

  5. Creating healthy competition among individuals and groups for the sake of growth is a value. «فآمن و استكبرتم» (They believed, and you acted arrogantly).

  6. Arrogance in the face of revelation and the Quran is oppression (zulm). «استكبرتم... القوم الظالمین»

  7. Oppression prevents insight and causes deprivation from divine favors. «انّ اللّه لا یهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَوْ كَانَ خَيْرًا مَّا سَبَقُونَآ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هَـٰذَآ إِفْكٌ قَدِيمٌ (۱۱)
The faithless say about the faithful, ‘Had it been [anything] good, they would not have taken the lead over us toward [accepting] it.’ And since they could not find the way to it, they will say, ‘It is an ancient lie.’ (11)

Key Points

The disbelievers imagine that only they understand, and that if there is something beneficial and good, they will attain it before others. And since they have not believed, necessarily, faith is not a good thing! After the Prophet said: «ما كنت بدعاً من الرسل» (meaning) the message is not a novelty, and I am not a newly emerged prophet, the enemies resorted to destroying the very principle of prophethood and said: «هذا افك قديم» (meaning) this is an ancient lie and an old deception.

Messages

1- The disbelievers consider themselves smarter than the Muslims. «لو كان خيراً ما سبقونا اليه»

2- Humiliating propaganda against Islam and Muslims has always existed. «لو كان خيراً ما سبقونا...»

3- There is no doubt about the guidance of the Quran, but stubborn and arrogant individuals do not accept it. «و اذ لم يهتدوا»

4- The enemy does not always enter through one path and does not have a single argument or logic. Sometimes they say: Islam has no value, otherwise we would have accepted it before everyone else, «لو كان خيراً ما سبقونا» and sometimes they say: These contents are nothing more than an old and long-standing lie. «فسیقولون هذا افك قديم»

5- Sometimes the educator must immunize those under his training by familiarizing them with various claims, tendencies, and poisonous, nonsensical statements, and by providing answers to them. «قال الّذين كفروا... فسیقولون...»

6- The leader and the people must anticipate the opponents' nonsense. «فسیقولون»

7- Opponents are so stubborn that they consider the content of the Quran a lie and also consider it expired (past its shelf life). «هذا افك قديم»

8- The notion that the Quran belonged to the time of the Prophet and is useless today is not a new doubt or statement; opponents who claimed to be thinkers during the Prophet's time also voiced these baseless doubts and statements. «هذا... قديم»

9- The ugliness of slander and fabrication is an innate matter. Even the disbelievers, in order to defeat Islam, called it ifk (slander) and fabrication. «افك قديم»

Ayah

وَمِن قَبْلِهِۦ كِتَـٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً ۚ وَهَـٰذَا كِتَـٰبٌ مُّصَدِّقٌ لِّسَانًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّيُنذِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۲)
Yet before it the Book of Moses was a guide and mercy, and this is a Book in the Arabic language, which confirms it, [sent] to warn the wrongdoers, and is a [bearer of] good news for the virtuous. (12)

Key Points

In Ayah 9, we read the statement of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله that his prophethood is not a novel or unprecedented phenomenon. This Ayah continues that theme and states: before the Qur'an, the Book of Moses was an Imam (a guide). Of course, before the Qur'an, the Gospel (Injil), the Book of Prophet Jesus, was also relevant. Perhaps the reason the Torah is specifically mentioned is that most of the Prophet's opponents in Medina were Jews. Furthermore, the Sharia (Law) of Prophet Jesus was a continuation of the Sharia of Prophet Moses, and the Gospel is a complement to the Torah. In the Qur'an, Prophet Abraham is presented as an Imam, «انّى جاعلك للنّاس اماما» (52), and the Book of Prophet Moses is considered an Imam, «كتاب موسى اماماً و رحمة». The conduct and method of the Prophet of Islam are introduced as a model, «لقد كان لكم فى رسول اللّه أسوة حسنة» (53). Believers also ask Allah for the leadership of the pious. «و اجعلنا للمتّقین اماماً» (54). Ayah 9 called the Prophet a Nadhir (warner), and this Ayah calls the Qur'an a Nadhir. «لینذر الّذین...» Just as Allah named the Prophet a Bashir (bearer of good news), «رسلا مبشّرین و منذرین» (55), this Ayah also names the Qur'an a Bashir. «بُشرى للمحسنین»

52) Al-Baqarah, 124. 53) Al-Ahzab, 21. 54) Al-Furqan, 74. 55) An-Nisa, 165.

Messages

1- Sending down heavenly Books is among the Divine traditions. «و من قبله كتاب موسى»

2- People need models and guides, and the heavenly Books, which are manifestations of Divine Mercy, are the Imams (guides) for the people. «كتاب موسى اماماً و رحمة»

3- Contrary to the rule of kings, which is often accompanied by domination and misguidance, religious leadership (Imamate) is mixed with guidance and mercy. «اماماً و رحمة»

4- When honoring the present, do not forget those who came before. «و من قبله... و هذا كتاب...»

5- Harmony and alignment among the heavenly Books are proof of their truthfulness. «و من قبله كتاب موسى... و هذا كتاب مصدق»

6- In the face of destruction and slander, honoring and affirmation are necessary. «هذا افك قدیم... هذا كتاب مصدّق»

7- By affirming the previous heavenly Books, the Qur'an holds a great right over them. «مصدّق»

8- The heavenly Book is a means of warning the oppressors and giving good news to the virtuous. «لینذر... و بشرى»

9- Accepting the heavenly Book is virtue (ihsan), and denying it is oppression against oneself. «ظلموا... محسنین»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا ٱللَّهُ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَقَـٰمُوا۟ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۱۳)
Those who say, ‘Our Lord is Allah,’ and then remain steadfast, they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (13)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤)
They shall be the inhabitants of paradise, remaining in it [forever]—a reward for what they used to do. (14)

Key Points

The intent behind saying "ربّنا اللّه" (Our Lord is Allah) is not merely the utterance, but rather choosing the path of truth and remaining steadfast upon it. In reality, more important than declaring faith and affirming Tawhid (monotheism) is steadfastness and persistence upon it. "ثمّ استقاموا" (then they remained steadfast). Khawf (fear) is dread and apprehension regarding the future, while Huzn (grief/sorrow) relates to the past. The word of truth must be declared, "قالوا ربّنا اللّه" (They said, 'Our Lord is Allah'); there must be continuity and steadfastness upon it, "ثم استقاموا" (then they remained steadfast); and it must be accompanied by action. "بما كانوا یعملون" (for what they used to do). Naturally, man desires tranquility, well-being, and eternal life, and Allah considers the path to achieving all these desires dependent upon faith and persistent righteous deeds. Steadfastness upon the truth is difficult, but it is made easy by a great reward, such as Paradise full of blessings and free from grief and sorrow. Therefore, difficult tasks must be compensated with great rewards.

Messages

1- We should not be arrogant about our current status, nor consider the mere declaration of faith sufficient; rather, we must consider steadfastness upon it, and the ultimate outcome and future. "قالوا ربّنا اللّه ثمّ استقاموا" (They said, 'Our Lord is Allah,' then they remained steadfast). (Just as Prophet Yusuf, after being saved from hardships and attaining the position of the Aziz of Egypt, said: "توفّنى مُسلماً والحقنى بالصالحین"(56) O God! Cause me to die as a Muslim and join me with the righteous.)

2- Faith in Allah eliminates fear of others. "ربّنا اللّه... لاخوف علیهم" (Our Lord is Allah... no fear shall be upon them).

3- Only those who constantly move on the path of Allah will not regret their past. "ربنّا اللّه ثم استقاموا... لا هم یحزنون" (Our Lord is Allah, then they remained steadfast... nor shall they grieve).

4- The reward for the firmness of faith and the continuity of good deeds is eternal residence in Paradise and its blessings. "استقاموا... خالدین فیها" (They remained steadfast... abiding eternally therein).

5- Paradise is the reward for the people of action, not the people of mere words. "جزاءً بما كانوا یعملون" (A reward for what they used to do).

(56) Yusuf, 101.

Ayah

وَوَصَّيْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ بِوَٰلِدَيْهِ إِحْسَـٰنًا ۖ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُۥ كُرْهًا وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهًا ۖ وَحَمْلُهُۥ وَفِصَـٰلُهُۥ ثَلَـٰثُونَ شَهْرًا ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُۥ وَبَلَغَ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً قَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِىٓ أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَىَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَىَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَـٰلِحًا تَرْضَىٰهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِى فِى ذُرِّيَّتِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّى مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۱٥)
We have enjoined man to be kind to his parents. His mother has carried him in travail, and bore him in travail, and his gestation and weaning take thirty months. When he comes of age and reaches forty years, he says, ‘My Lord! Inspire me to give thanks for Your blessing with which You have blessed me and my parents, and that I may do righteous deeds which please You, and invest my descendants with righteousness. Indeed I have turned to you in penitence, and I am one of the Muslims.’ (15)

Key Points

"Kurh" (كُره) means internal hardship, while "karh" (كَره) means external hardship. (57) "Awzi'ni" (أوزعنى) means creating desire and love for performing an action, which is referred to as Divine success (Tawfiq). Kindness (Ihsan) to parents holds a special status with Allah. The word "waṣṣaynā" (We enjoined) is used five times in the Quran, three of which relate to parents. (58) This Ayah refers to the period from the human fetus and infancy up to the age of forty, and this age is considered the peak of human physical perfection. "balagha ashuddahu wa balagha arba'īna sanatan" (He reached his full strength and reached forty years). It is understood from this Ayah that the shortest period of pregnancy is six months, because if two years of breastfeeding are subtracted from the thirty months mentioned in the Ayah, the minimum duration of gestation becomes six months. "wa ḥamluhu wa fiṣāluhu thalāthūna shahran" (and his bearing and his weaning are thirty months). Due to the difficulties of pregnancy, childbirth, and breastfeeding, the mother has a special right over the child, and greater kindness must be shown to her. As such, traditions (Riwāyāt) place greater emphasis on kindness to the mother, and the Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) in one tradition advised kindness to the mother three times, and then advised kindness to the father. (59)

57) Mufradāt al-Rāghib. 58) Al-'Ankabūt (29): 8, Luqmān (31): 14, and this very Ayah. 59) Tafsir Namūneh and Al-Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 159.

Messages

1- The injunction to be kind to parents has existed throughout history and in all religions. ("waṣṣaynā" is in the past tense).

2- Kindness to parents is a human right that every father and mother holds over their child. "waṣṣaynā al-insāna" (We enjoined man) (It did not say: "We enjoined those who believed").

3- In showing kindness to parents, their being Muslim is not a condition. "waṣṣaynā al-insāna biwālidayhi" (We enjoined man concerning his parents).

4- The strengthening of the family foundation and the preservation of the parents' status are of special concern to Allah. "waṣṣaynā al-insāna biwālidayhi iḥsānan" (We enjoined man concerning his parents, [to show] kindness).

5- Not only spending (infaq), assistance, and respect, but every type of goodness toward parents is necessary. ("iḥsānan" is indefinite, encompassing every type of kindness).

6- Kindness to parents must be continuous and outstanding, not merely necessary or temporary. (The tanwīn [double vowel marker] on "iḥsānan" is a sign of greatness and prominence).

7- Paying attention to Allah's strict injunction regarding the past efforts of parents makes showing kindness and serving them more sincere and serious. "waṣṣaynā al-insāna... ḥamalat'hu ummuhu" (We enjoined man... his mother bore him).

8- The perfection of kindness to parents lies in performing it directly (without intermediary). "biwālidayni iḥsānan" (The letter 'bā' in "biwālidayni" indicates that the kindness to parents should be done by one's own hand).

9- Remembering the suffering and efforts of mothers is effective in arousing the children's affection. "ḥamalat'hu ummuhu kurhan... kurhan..." (His mother bore him with hardship... hardship...).

10- The mother's right is greater than the father's. Following the word "parents" (wālidayn), the discussion turns to the mother's suffering. "ḥamalat'hu ummuhu kurhan..." (His mother bore him with hardship...).

11- All religious commands, orders, and prohibitions have a reason and philosophy (although the reason for some, like kindness to parents, is stated). "waṣṣaynā... ḥamalat'hu ummuhu kurhan..." (We enjoined... his mother bore him with hardship...).

12- The age of forty is the peak of human capability and growth. "balagha ashuddahu wa balagha arba'īna sanatan" (He reached his full strength and reached forty years).

13- Divine gratitude requires seeking assistance and success (Tawfiq) from Allah. "awzi'nī an ashkura ni'mataka" (Enable me to be grateful for Your favor).

14- A portion of man's blessings comes through his parents and ancestors. "an'amta 'alayya wa 'alā wālidayya" (You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents).

15- Physical maturity and intellectual perfection are the foundation for spiritual maturity. "balagha ashuddahu wa balagha arba'īna sanatan qāla rabbi..." (He reached his full strength and reached forty years, he said, 'My Lord...').

16- A child's gratitude for the blessings Allah has given his parents is a duty and a form of kindness to the parents. "ashkura ni'mataka... 'alā wālidayya" (I be grateful for Your favor... upon my parents).

17- The best way to thank Allah for His blessings is to perform good deeds. "ashkura ni'mataka... a'mala ṣāliḥan" (I be grateful for Your favor... I do righteousness).

18- The value of a righteous deed is that it is continuous and permanent. ("a'mala," the imperfect verb, is a sign of permanence and continuity).

19- A righteous deed is valuable when it is sincere and receives Allah's acceptance and pleasure. "a'mala ṣāliḥan tarḍāhu" (I do righteousness that You approve).

20- A righteous child and generation are a source of pride for the parents. "aṣliḥ lī fī dhurriyyatī" (Make righteous for me my offspring).

21- The best supplication is one in which a person pays attention both to his parents and to his children. "'alā wālidayya... aṣliḥ lī fī dhurriyyatī" (upon my parents... Make righteous for me my offspring).

22- We must repent and compensate for the ingratitude we have shown toward Allah and our parents. "wa 'alā wālidayya... innī tubtu ilayka" (and upon my parents... indeed, I have repented to You).

23- Living in submission to Allah's command and dying as a Muslim is the supplication of the pure and the righteous. "innī tubtu ilayka wa innī min al-muslimīn" (indeed, I have repented to You, and indeed, I am of the Muslims).

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ نَتَقَبَّلُ عَنْهُمْ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَنَتَجَاوَزُ عَن سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ فِىٓ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَعْدَ ٱلصِّدْقِ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ (۱٦)
Such are the ones from whom We accept the best of what they do, and overlook their misdeeds, [who will be] among the inhabitants of paradise—a true promise which they had been given. (16)

Key Points

The acceptance of deeds has levels: a) Ordinary acceptance. «انما یتقبّل اللّه من المتّقین» (60) Allah accepts only from the pious. b) Good acceptance. «تقبّلها ربّها بقبول حسن» (61) Her Lord accepted Maryam with a good acceptance. c) Best acceptance. «نتقبّل عنهم احسن ما عملوا» (We accept from them the best of what they did) and in supplication, we read: O God, accept it from us in the best way. «فتقبله منّا باحسن قبولك» (62) If a person's deeds are not accepted by the Truth (Allah), even building the House of God, the Ka'bah, is useless. When Prophet Ibrahim was raising the foundations of the Ka'bah, he did not boast about himself or his work, but rather said: «ربّنا تقبل منّا» (63) Our Lord! Accept it from us. Human deeds have various types: obligatory (wajib), forbidden (haram), recommended (mustahabb), disliked (makruh), and permissible (mubah). On the Day of Judgment, Allah accepts the best of these five types of deeds—which are the obligatory and recommended acts—from the believers, and He overlooks their bad deeds, whether disliked or forbidden. Permissible acts are not subject to rejection or acceptance. (64) A person breaks His promise either because He was inattentive when making the promise and spoke hastily, or because He became weak or regretful afterward. Attributing any of these flaws or characteristics to Allah, Who is All-Knowing, Capable, and Powerful, is incorrect.

(60) Al-Ma'idah, 27. (61) Aal-i-Imran, 37. (62) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 165. (63) Al-Baqarah, 127. (64) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan and Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The value of every deed lies in its acceptance. «أولئك الّذین نتقبّل عنهم...»

2- Kindness to parents and gratitude to Allah are the prerequisites for the acceptance of a person's righteous deeds. «بوالدیه احساناً... اشكر نعمتك... اولئك الّذین نتقبل عنهم»

3- In the Divine presence, even a repentant sinner is accepted. «اولئك الذین نتقبل عنهم... و نتجاوز عن سیئاتهم»

4- Kindness to parents and Divine gratitude are the basis for the acceptance of good deeds and benefiting from Divine forgiveness. «احساناً... اشكر نعمتك... نتجاوز عن سیئاتهم»

5- Kindness to parents and Divine gratitude are the key to Paradise. «احسانا... اشكر نعمتك... فى اصحاب الجنة»

6- Divine promises are inviolable. «وعد الصدق»

7- Prophets and their successors constantly convey the Divine messages and promises to the people. «كانوا یوعدون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِى قَالَ لِوَٰلِدَيْهِ أُفٍّ لَّكُمَآ أَتَعِدَانِنِىٓ أَنْ أُخْرَجَ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ ٱلْقُرُونُ مِن قَبْلِى وَهُمَا يَسْتَغِيثَانِ ٱللَّهَ وَيْلَكَ ءَامِنْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ فَيَقُولُ مَا هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۷)
As for him who says to his parents, ‘Fie on you! Do you promise me that I shall be raised [from the dead] when generations have passed away before me?’ And they invoke Allah’s help [and say]: ‘Woe to you! Believe! Indeed Allah’s promise is true.’ But he says, ‘These are nothing but myths of the ancients.’ (17)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَوْلُ فِىٓ أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ (۱۸)
Such are the ones against whom the word has become due, along with the nations of jinn and humans that have passed away before them. They were the losers. (18)

Key Points

"Uff" (أُفّ) means declaring aversion, expressing displeasure, and is a form of insult, humiliation, and disrespect. In the previous Ayah, the effects of kindness to parents, faith in Allah, and gratitude for divine blessings—which is eternal Paradise—were discussed. In this Ayah, the character of the unworthy child and his ominous and ruinous fate are depicted so that the manner of speech, behavior, and ultimate end of the two types of children can be compared. The purpose of the gathering (Hashr) of people in the Hereafter is the resurrection of all people at one time and place, not that groups of the dead are revived in this world every now and then, which is why this unworthy child asks his parents: Why have the previous generations not been revived? Whoever does not believe in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) trades his existence, talents, and blessings for a meager price and fleeting goals, and on the Day of Judgment, he will be empty-handed, his capital wasted, and he will be among those who regret and suffer loss.

Messages

  1. The unworthy child speaks ill of and is disrespectful to his parents: «اُفّ لكما» (Uff upon you two), but the faithful and compassionate parents seek help from Allah for the guidance of their child and also enjoin him to good and forbid him from evil. «یستغیثان اللّه ویلك آمن» (They seek Allah's help, [saying]: Woe to you! Believe!)

  2. Usually, the denier of the Resurrection has neither argument nor manners. «اُفّ لكما أتعداننى» (Uff upon you two! Do you promise me...)

  3. The child is free, independent, and responsible for his own actions. «أتعداننى أن اخرج... ویلك آمن» (Do you promise me that I will be brought forth... Woe to you! Believe!)

  4. Parents are responsible for guiding and instructing their children, even if they do not receive a positive response. «أتعداننى ان اخرج... ویلك آمن»

  5. Parents are responsible for the spiritual growth of their child, alongside his physical growth and nourishment. «حملته... بلغ اشدّه... هما یستغیثان... ویلك آمن» (She bore him... he reached his maturity... they seek help... Woe to you! Believe!)

  6. Parents should educate their children by reminding them of the Resurrection and encouraging foresight regarding the outcome. «آمن انّ وعد اللّه حق» (Believe! Indeed, the promise of Allah is true.)

  7. Lack of faith in the Resurrection leads to the child's lack of commitment, even towards his parents. «اُفّ لكما... ما هذا الا اساطیر الاولین» (Uff upon you two... This is nothing but the tales of the ancients.)

  8. Deniers of the Resurrection consider believers to be reactionary and rigid, while viewing themselves as progressive thinkers. «ما هذا الاّ اساطیر الاولین» (This is nothing but the tales of the ancients.)

  9. Disbelief in the Resurrection, speaking ill, and showing impudent disrespect towards parents leads to certain punishment. «قال لوالدیه افّ... اولئك الّذین حق علیهم القول» (He said to his parents, Uff... Those are the ones against whom the decree has been justified.)

  10. The children's obstinacy in disbelief prevents the acceptance of their parents' prayers on their behalf. «و هما یستغیثان اللّه... حقّ علیهم القول» (And they seek Allah's help... the decree has been justified against them.)

  11. Allah's tradition (Sunnah) is the same for all deviants. «حق علیهم القول فى امم قد خلت من قبلهم» (The decree has been justified against them among the communities that passed away before them.)

  12. Disbelief and denial of the Resurrection have a long history among the jinn and mankind. «اُمم قد خلت من قبلهم من الجن و الانس» (Communities that passed away before them, of the jinn and mankind.)

  13. The jinn are also beings like humans; they have duties (taklif), civilization, death and mortality, accountability, and receive punishment and reward. «امم... من الجنّ و الانس» (Communities... of the jinn and mankind.)

  14. Denial of the Resurrection and intellectual deviation lead to heavy loss. «انهم كانوا خاسرین» (Indeed, they were losers.)

  15. Impudence towards parents, even by uttering the smallest word, is a great crime, condemned by the Quran, and a source of human loss. «اُفّ لكما... كانوا خاسرین» (Uff upon you two... they were losers.)

Ayah

وَلِكُلٍّ دَرَجَـٰتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا۟ ۖ وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۱۹)
For everyone there are degrees [of merit] pertaining to what he has done: He will recompense them fully for their works, and they will not be wronged. (19)

Key Points

The word "Liwaffiyahum" (لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ) is derived from wafa (وَفَىٰ), meaning complete reception. This means that the punishment, reward, and ranks of individuals on the Day of Judgment will be given completely. In this world, the possibility of complete and full punishment and reward does not exist, because this world is both very small and limited, and the effects and consequences of worldly recompense spread to others, causing them distress and harm. What reward do we have for the Prophets who saved millions of people from ignorance, polytheism (shirk), discord, corruption, and various deviations? Even the best worldly blessings, such as food, clothing, and means of comfort, are nothing, because they are also available to the tyrants (Taghuts). Similarly, what punishment do we have for criminals and executioners who have killed or wounded thousands of people, other than executing them, which is a very insignificant and unjust penalty?

Messages

1- Felicity and wretchedness have degrees and ranks that a person attains through his deeds. «لِكُلٍّ دَرَجَاتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا»

2- The criterion for determining rank and status on the Day of Judgment is action (deed). «لِكُلٍّ دَرَجَاتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا»

3- One of the goals of establishing the Day of Judgment is the accounting of people's deeds. «وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَالَهُمْ»

4- The Day of Judgment is a reward for the believers and a punishment for the evildoers. «لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَالَهُمْ»

5- With the passage of time, human deeds are not erased or destroyed; rather, they are fully embodied on the Day of Judgment. «لِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَالَهُمْ» And the reward and recompense will be given without the slightest deficiency. «وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ أَذْهَبْتُمْ طَيِّبَـٰتِكُمْ فِى حَيَاتِكُمُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱسْتَمْتَعْتُم بِهَا فَٱلْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذَابَ ٱلْهُونِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَفْسُقُونَ (۲٠)
The day when the faithless are exposed to the Fire, [they will be told,] ‘You have exhausted your good things in the life of the world and enjoyed them. So today you will be requited with a humiliating punishment for your acting arrogantly in the earth unduly, and because you used to transgress.’ (20)

Key Points

The loss of opportunities and blessings, and regretting them, begins from the moment of death. Imam Ali عليه السلام says regarding the manner of giving up the soul: In the final moments of life, man thinks about what he has done with his life and how he has spent his time. «یفكّر فیم افنى عمره و فیم اذهب دهره»(65) In the Quran, verses depict this state, stating that man says at the time of death: «ما اغنى عنّى مالیه . هلك عنّى سلطانیه»(66) My wealth was not useful to me; my power and strength are gone. In this Ayah too, people are told: You wasted the good things (Tayyibat) and made no provision. On the Day of Judgment, sometimes the criminals are presented to the Fire, «یعرض الّذین كفروا على النّار», and sometimes the Fire is presented to them. «و عرضنا جهنم یومئذ للكافرین»(67) And in another stage, the criminals are thrown into it. «خذوه فغلّوه ثمّ الجحیم صلّوه»(68)

65) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 109. 66) Al-Haqqah, 28 - 29. 67) Al-Kahf, 100. 68) Al-Haqqah, 30 - 31.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there are various types of physical, spiritual, and psychological tortures for the criminals. «یعرض الّذین كفروا على النار أذهبتم» (The punishment of the criminals is accompanied by rebuke, reproach, and humiliation.)

2- It is lawful for man to use blessings and good things (Tayyibat), «اُحلّ لكم الطیّبات»(69), but consuming them in pursuit of desires is a loss. «اذهبتم طیّباتكم»

3- The recompense for arrogance (Takabbur) in this world is disgrace and humiliation in the Hereafter. «عذاب الهون بما كنتم تستكبرون»

4- Haughtiness and arrogance (Istikbar) are the prelude to transgression (Fisq) and deviation from the path of truth. «تستكبرون... تفسقون»

5- Inner arrogance is the cause of bad external performance. «تستكبرون... تفسقون» 69) Al-Ma'idah, 4.

Ayah

۞ وَٱذْكُرْ أَخَا عَادٍ إِذْ أَنذَرَ قَوْمَهُۥ بِٱلْأَحْقَافِ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ ٱلنُّذُرُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦٓ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۲۱)
And mention [Hud] the brother of ‘Ad, when he warned his people at Ahqaf—and warners have passed away before and after him—saying, ‘Do not worship anyone but Allah. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (21)

Key Points

"Al-Ahqaf" (الأحقاف) is derived from hiqf (حقف), meaning shifting sand. It is the name of a sandy, dune-filled region in Southern Arabia, located between Yemen, Oman, and Aden, which was the dwelling place of the people of 'Ad. (70) The expression "brother," which is used for Prophets such as Salih, Shu'ayb, and Hud (71)—Prophet Hud being called akha 'Ad (the brother of 'Ad)—is either due to the familial connection of these Prophets with their people, or due to the brotherly motivation and zeal of the Prophets for guiding the people. We read in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn: During the time of Al-Mu'tasim al-'Abbasi, they dug a well in the Batha'iniyyah region of Iraq to access water. Although they dug a deep well, they did not reach water and abandoned it. During the time of Al-Mutawakkil, they resumed digging the well until they reached a hard slab of rock. When they broke it, suddenly a cold wind blew, destroying a number of people. They asked Imam Al-Hadi عليه السلام about this strange incident. He replied: That was the land of Al-Ahqaf and the polytheistic people of Hud, who were destroyed by the blowing of a cold wind.

70) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Qur'an. 71) Hud, 50, 61, 84.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of past generations and recalling important historical events is a source of lesson and admonition. «واذكر...»

2- The Prophets treated people with brotherly conduct. «اخا عاد»

3- When recounting history, we should omit unnecessary preliminaries and focus on the secrets and mysteries behind movements, growth, and downfall. «و اذكر... اذ انذر...»

4- The method of the Prophets when dealing with the deviated was warning. «اذ انذر»

5- The first audience of the Prophets were their own relatives and people. «قومه»

6- Difficult geographical and environmental conditions were not an obstacle to the work of the Prophets. «بالاحقاف»

7- It was among the Divine traditions (Sunan) of Allah to send multiple Prophets. «خلت النذر من بین یدیه و من خلفه»

8- The foremost priority in the work of the Prophets was the invitation to monotheism. «الاّ تعبدوا الاّ اللّه»

9- The best method of invitation is reminding people of the consequences of their actions and having foresight. «انى اخاف علیكم عذاب یوم عظیم»

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَأْفِكَنَا عَنْ ءَالِهَتِنَا فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَآ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۲۲)
They said, ‘Have you come to turn us away from our gods? Then bring us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful. (22)
قَالَ إِنَّمَا ٱلْعِلْمُ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ وَأُبَلِّغُكُم مَّآ أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِۦ وَلَـٰكِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُمْ قَوْمًا تَجْهَلُونَ (۲۳)
He said, ‘The knowledge [of when that threat will be carried out] is with Allah alone. I communicate to you what I have been sent with. But I see that you are a senseless lot.’ (23)

Messages

1- The Prophets did not wait for people to approach them; they went to the people themselves, and usually, they were not met with a warm reception. «أجئتنا»

2- Sometimes polytheism and idol worship appear so much like the truth that Monotheism (Tawhid) is considered a deviation. «لتافكنا عن آلهتنا»

3- Knowledge of all existential realities belongs exclusively to Allah. «انّما العلم عند اللّه»

4- The Prophets also had limitations in knowledge and acknowledged this. «انّما العلم عند اللّه» (Sometimes, admitting "I do not know" is necessary.)

5- The duty of the Prophets is the conveyance of the Divine message. «ابلّغكم ما ارسلت به»

6- The inappropriate request for the descent of punishment, instead of reflecting upon the truth, is a sign of ignorance and foolishness. «فأتنا بما تعدنا... أراكم قوماً تجهلون»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ عَارِضًا مُّسْتَقْبِلَ أَوْدِيَتِهِمْ قَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَا عَارِضٌ مُّمْطِرُنَا ۚ بَلْ هُوَ مَا ٱسْتَعْجَلْتُم بِهِۦ ۖ رِيحٌ فِيهَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۲٤)
When they saw it as a cloud advancing toward their valleys, they said, ‘This cloud brings us rain.’ ‘No, it is what you sought to hasten: a hurricane carrying a painful punishment, (24)
تُدَمِّرُ كُلَّ شَىْءٍۭ بِأَمْرِ رَبِّهَا فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ لَا يُرَىٰٓ إِلَّا مَسَـٰكِنُهُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۲٥)
destroying everything by its Lord’s command.’ So they became such that nothing could be seen except their dwellings. Thus do We requite the guilty lot. (25)

Key Points

The meaning of 'āriḍ (عارض) is a mass of clouds that appears in the sky, and its expanse covers the entire horizon. Awdiya (أودیة) is the plural of wādī (وادى), meaning a valley or a land of habitation. Tudammir (تدمّر) is derived from tadmir (تدمیر), meaning to destroy and annihilate. The people of 'Ād, who were facing severe drought and lack of water and had been waiting for rain for a long time, were initially happy upon seeing the mass of clouds. However, they later realized that this cloud was not a cloud of blessing, but rather a cloud of retribution and the very punishment they had desired and hastened its arrival.

Messages

1- The descent of Allah's wrath occurs after the completion of the argument (proof). «ابلّغكم... ریح فیها عذاب»

2- Some clouds are not rain-bearing; rather, they cause severe and terrifying storms. Upon seeing the clouds, the polytheists said: «هذا عارض ممطرنا» (This is a cloud that will give us rain), but Prophet Hūd said: «بل هو... ریح فیها عذاب» (Nay, it is... a wind wherein is a painful punishment).

3- Nature is both a path for Allah's grace, «ارسلنا الریاح لواقح»(72), and it can also be a means of Allah's wrath. «ریح فیها عذاب الیم»

4- The painful punishment ('adhāb alīm) is not exclusive to the Hereafter; it can also occur in this world. «ریح فیها عذاب الیم»

5- If Allah's wrath arrives, nothing can prevent it. «تدمّر كل شى ء»

6- The movement of clouds and winds is also not accidental; rather, it is by His Divine command. «بامر ربّها»

7- The remnants left behind by the criminals are an exhibition of warning (a lesson). «لایرى الاّ مساكنهم»

8- The uprooting and destruction of criminals is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «كذلك»

9- The cause of people's destruction is their own behavior. «نجزى المجرمین»72) Hajar, 22.

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّـٰهُمْ فِيمَآ إِن مَّكَّنَّـٰكُمْ فِيهِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ سَمْعًا وَأَبْصَـٰرًا وَأَفْـِٔدَةً فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَلَآ أَبْصَـٰرُهُمْ وَلَآ أَفْـِٔدَتُهُم مِّن شَىْءٍ إِذْ كَانُوا۟ يَجْحَدُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (۲٦)
Certainly We had granted them power in respects that We have not granted you, and We had vested them with hearing and sight and hearts. But neither their hearing availed them in any way nor did their sight, nor their hearts when they used to impugn the signs of Allah. So they were besieged by what they used to deride. (26)

Messages

1- Although human effort is necessary to acquire resources, all of them belong to Allah. «و لقد مكنّاهم»

2- When superpowers collapse, others must take account of their own affairs. «تدمّر كل شى ء... و لقد مكنّاهم»

3- Resources and wealth are not always a source of growth. «مكناهم... فما اغنى عنهم»

4- If man's will is not inclined toward accepting admonition, neither material resources nor physical abilities will be effective. «ما اغنى عنهم سمعهم... اذ كانوا یجحدون»

5- Material wealth and resources are valuable only if they are in the hands of a righteous person; otherwise, those very resources become the cause of man's torment and suffering. «مكّناهم... فما اغنى عنهم سمعهم... اذ كانوا یجحدون»

6- The denial and mockery of the disbelievers lead to their destruction. «كانوا یجحدون... كانوا به یستهزئون»

7- Allah's punishments are proportionate to the type of man's action and behavior. «حاق بهم ما كانوا به یستهزئون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَا مَا حَوْلَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَصَرَّفْنَا ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۲۷)
Certainly We have destroyed the towns that were around you, and We have variously paraphrased the signs so that they may come back. (27)

Messages

1- Divine destruction and wrath occur after the completion of the argument (proof). «اهلكنا... صرّفنا»

2- We should study the history of the nations near us, as this has a greater impact. «ما حولكم من القرى»

3- There must be variety in propagation (preaching). «صرّفنا الآیات»

4- Human growth is achieved through returning to the innate nature (fitrah) and the path of Allah. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

فَلَوْلَا نَصَرَهُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ قُرْبَانًا ءَالِهَةًۢ ۖ بَلْ ضَلُّوا۟ عَنْهُمْ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ إِفْكُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۲۸)
So why did not those [fake deities] help them whom they had taken as gods besides Allah, as a means of nearness [to Him]? Indeed, they forsook them; that was their lie and what they used to fabricate. (28)

Messages

1- One must learn a lesson from the weakness and uselessness of deities other than Allah. «فلولا نصرهم»

2- The inclination towards divine proximity is innate, and even idolaters considered idols a means of drawing closer to God. «قرباناً آلهة»

3- We should rely on someone who is present and active on the day of need and hardship, not absent and ineffective. «بل ضلّوا عنهم»

4- The help provided by idols is a false, baseless, and illusory fantasy. «ذلك افكهم و ما كانوا یفترون»

Ayah

وَإِذْ صَرَفْنَآ إِلَيْكَ نَفَرًا مِّنَ ٱلْجِنِّ يَسْتَمِعُونَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ فَلَمَّا حَضَرُوهُ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنصِتُوا۟ ۖ فَلَمَّا قُضِىَ وَلَّوْا۟ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِم مُّنذِرِينَ (۲۹)
When We dispatched toward you a team of jinn listening to the Quran, when they were in its presence, they said, ‘Be silent!’ When it was finished, they went back to their people as warners. (29)

Key Points

Familiarity with the Quran opens and broadens the insight and horizon of the wise. The Quran states: The being possessing consciousness and will is not only man, but another being, like the Jinn, possesses greater capability while having the same conditions. Perhaps in this Ayah there is an allusion (or reproach) to the polytheistic and deviated humans who consider themselves the ultimate intellect: that if you did not believe upon hearing all these verses, the Jinn, however, upon hearing just a few verses, both believed and became its propagators.

Messages

1- Wherever Allah wills, the course of movements changes. «صرفنا»

2- The Jinn, like humans, possess intellect, obligation (taklif), responsibility, the power of choice, and the ability to guide their own kind. «یستمعون القرآن...»

3- The Jinn also understand that silence must be observed when listening to the Quran. «یستمعون القرآن... قالوا انصتوا»

4- After recognizing the truth, action is necessary. «یستمعون القرآن... ولّوا الى قومهم»

5- In propagation (Dawah), one must start with acquaintances and close relatives. «الى قومهم»

6- Wherever there is talent and readiness, a few verses and a few reminders are effective. «یستمعون القرآن... ولّوا الى قومهم منذرین»

7- The Jinn also possess social life. «نفراً من الجنّ... ولّوا الى قومهم منذرین»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ يَـٰقَوْمَنَآ إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا كِتَـٰبًا أُنزِلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مُوسَىٰ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلْحَقِّ وَإِلَىٰ طَرِيقٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۳٠)
They said, ‘O our people! Indeed, we have heard a Book, which has been sent down after Moses, confirming what was before it. It guides to the truth and to a straight path. (30)

Messages

1- The Prophet of mankind is also the Prophet of the jinn, and a group of jinn are tasked with propagating the religion and the way of the Prophets among their own community. «قالوا یا قومنا انّا سمعنا...»

2- The jinn are aware of the history of the Prophets and the heavenly Books. «كتاباً انزل من بعد موسى»

3- The jinn are aware of the content of the heavenly Books. «مصدّقا لما بین یدیه»

4- Harmony among the heavenly Books is a sign of their truthfulness. «مصدّقا لما بین یدیه»

5- The jinn understood the truth (Haqq) upon hearing a few verses, but humans like Abu Jahl and Abu Lahab spent a lifetime with the Prophet and refused to understand the truth. «یهدى الى الحق»

6- The path of truth is the straight path (sirāt al-mustaqīm). It is a path based on moderation, far from any form of extremism or negligence. «یهدى الى الحق و الى طریق مستقیم»

Ayah

يَـٰقَوْمَنَآ أَجِيبُوا۟ دَاعِىَ ٱللَّهِ وَءَامِنُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَغْفِرْ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُجِرْكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۳۱)
O our people! Respond to Allah’s summoner and have faith in Him. He will forgive you some of your sins and shelter you from a painful punishment.’ (31)

Key Points

In the Quran, faith (Iman), seeking forgiveness (Istighfar), benevolent loans (Qard al-Hasanah), and piety (Taqwa) are among the factors that attract Divine forgiveness (Maghfirah). In this Ayah, all the fundamental principles of Islamic belief (Usul al-Din) are mentioned: Monotheism (Tawhid): «آمنوا به» (believe in Him), Prophethood (Nubuwwah): «داعى اللّه» (the caller to Allah), and Resurrection (Ma'ad): «یجركم من عذاب الیم» (He will protect you from a painful punishment).

Messages

1- In propagating the religion, we should utilize the element of emotion and social bonds. «یا قومنا» (O our people) is repeated twice.

2- Prophets invite people to Allah, not towards themselves. «داعى اللّه» (the caller to Allah).

3- In propagation and enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf), we should explain the consequences of actions. «اجیبوا... یغفر لكم» (Respond... He will forgive you).

4- Faith removes some sins, such as disbelief (Kufr) and polytheism (Shirk), but other sins, such as violating people's rights, are treated differently. «من ذنوبكم» (The word min [من], means that a portion of your sins will be forgiven.)

5- Jinn, like humans, are accountable (Mukallaf), and in case of disobedience, they are considered sinners and are subject to wrath and punishment. «ذنوبكم... عذاب الیم» (your sins... painful punishment).

Ayah

وَمَن لَّا يُجِبْ دَاعِىَ ٱللَّهِ فَلَيْسَ بِمُعْجِزٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَيْسَ لَهُۥ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءُ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳۲)
Those who do not respond to Allah’s summoner cannot frustrate [Allah] on the earth, and they will not find any protectors besides Him. They are in manifest error. (32)

Messages

1- The Jinn possess the power of choice and free will. «اجیبوا... و من لا یجب»

2- Encouragement and threat must be presented side by side. «یغفر لكم... فلیس بمعجز»

3- Disbelief places man in a dead end: he cannot escape by himself, «لیس بمعجز فى الارض...» nor can anyone else release him. «لیس له من دونه اولیاء»

4- Every path other than the path of Allah is manifest error. «من لا یحب... فى ضلال مبین»

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَلَمْ يَعْىَ بِخَلْقِهِنَّ بِقَـٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يُحْـِۧىَ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ ۚ بَلَىٰٓ إِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۳۳)
Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and [who] was not exhausted by their creation, is able to revive the dead? Yes, indeed He has power over all things. (33)

Key Points

Lamyu'yi (لم یعى) is derived from ‘iyy (عىّ), meaning inability. If we consider this Ayah to be a continuation of the Jinn's speech, it becomes clear that the discussion regarding the denial of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) also exists among them.

Messages

1- For the possibility of the Resurrection, let us look at the manifestation of Allah's power in existence. «اولم یروا... انه على كل شى قدیر»

2- Allah's power is absolute and unlimited. «و لم یعى بخلقهن»

3- Do the deniers of the Resurrection consider the revival of the dead harder than the creation of the heavens and the earth, such that they do not accept it? «خلق السموات و الارض... بقادر على ان یحیى الموتى»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ أَلَيْسَ هَـٰذَا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّنَا ۚ قَالَ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (۳٤)
The day when the faithless are exposed to the Fire, [He will say,] ‘Is this not a fact?’ They will say, ‘Yes, by our Lord!’ He will say, ‘So taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.’ (34)

Key Points

In Ayah 20, the issue of presenting the disbelievers to the Fire was also mentioned. There, they were reprimanded: «اذهبتم طیّباتكم فى الحیاة الدنیا» (You exhausted your good things in the life of the world) because you lost your blessings in the world and did not save any provision. But in this Ayah, they are questioned: «ألیس هذا بالحق» (Is this not the truth)?

Messages

  1. In the Hereafter, physical, spiritual, and psychological torment occur simultaneously. «یعرض... ألیس هذا بالحق»

  2. On the Day of Judgment, the disbelievers will confess to the Lordship of Allah and the truthfulness of the Resurrection. «بلى و ربّنا»

  3. In the Hereafter, acknowledging and confessing the truth is of no benefit. «قالوا بلى و ربّنا... فذوقوا...»

Ayah

فَٱصْبِرْ كَمَا صَبَرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْعَزْمِ مِنَ ٱلرُّسُلِ وَلَا تَسْتَعْجِل لَّهُمْ ۚ كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ مَا يُوعَدُونَ لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّن نَّهَارٍۭ ۚ بَلَـٰغٌ ۚ فَهَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (۳٥)
So be patient just as the resolute among the apostles were patient, and do not seek to hasten [the punishment] for them. The day when they see what they are promised, [it will be] as though they had remained [in the world] only an hour of a day. This is a proclamation. So shall anyone be destroyed except the transgressing lot? (35)

Key Points

‘Azm’ (resolve) means both intention and decision, and also ‘azīmah’ (determination) and Sharī‘ah (Divine Law). The meaning of ‘Ulū al-‘Azm’ is the Prophets who possessed Divine Law. The Ulū al-‘Azm Prophets are: 1. Prophet Noah 2. Prophet Abraham 3. Prophet Moses 4. Prophet Jesus 5. Prophet Muhammad, as mentioned in Ayah 7 of Sūrat al-Ahzāb and Ayah 13 of Sūrat ash-Shūrā: «شرع لكم من الدّین ما وصّى به نوحا و الّذى اوحینا الیك و ما وصینا به ابراهیم و موسى و عیسى» The Quran mentions many verses regarding the patience (sabr) of the Prophets, including: «و لقد كذّبت رسل من قبلك فصَبروا على ما كذّبوا و اوذوا حتّى اتاهم نصرنا»(73) Indeed, the Messengers before you were subjected to persecution and denial, but they remained patient until Our Divine help arrived. Another Ayah considers all the Prophets to be among the patient ones. «كلٌّ من الصابرین»(74)

73) Al-An‘ām, 34. 74) Al-Anbiyā’, 85.

Messages

1- The obstinacy, persecution, and harassment of the disbelievers against the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله were so severe that the Prophet needed comfort, consolation, and counsel from Allah. «فاصبر كما صبر...»

2- Attention to the punishment of the disbelievers prepares the ground for the patience and steadfastness of the believers. «و یوم یعرض الذین كفروا... فاصبر»

3- Man needs role models, and role models play an effective role in his growth and development. «فاصبر كما صبر أولوا العزم»

4- Not all Prophets are of the same rank, «أولوا العزم من الرّسل» (Those of strong resolve among the Messengers), and their patience was not identical. «كما صبر أولوا العزم»

5- The Prophet of Islam is also one of the Ulū al-‘Azm Prophets; otherwise, commanding him to have patience like the patience of those Prophets would not have been appropriate for him. «فاصبر كما صبر أولوا العزم»

6- The obstinacy and stubbornness of the disbelievers were so great that they might compel the one possessing ‘Khuluqin ‘Aẓīm’ (sublime character) and ‘Raḥmatun lil-‘Ālamīn’ (a mercy to the worlds) to request punishment. (Although this request is not explicitly stated.) «لاتستعجل لهم»

7- A leader must possess broad-mindedness (patience and tolerance). «فاصبر... و لاتستعجل لهم»

8- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah to grant respite to people. «و لاتستعجل لهم»

9- The lifespan of this world, compared to the Hereafter, is like an hour of a day. «لم یلبثوا الاّ ساعة من نهار»

10- The Quran is the best means of propagation; rather, it is the very essence and peak of the message (balāgh). «بلاغ»

11- Transgression (fisq) is the cause of ruin and the source of destruction for the individual and society. «فهل یهلك الا القوم الفاسقون»
